growth_records.notes: growth_rate_per_h computed as ln(2)/doubling_time_h. Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

growth_records.medium: mBG-11 (BP-1 medium), autotrophic (CO2/HCO3- sole C source), nitrate, no vitamins

up.method: reported as specific growth rate on a Cmol basis in Fig 1A (numeric values only in figure, not in text)
nitrate sole N source in defined medium; no numeric uptake reported in text

sec.method: reported in Fig 1B as specific rate of net O2 production; numeric values only in figure

yields.note: photosynthetic quotient (Cmol fixed into biomass per mol O2 produced) reported qualitatively/graphically in Fig 1C; no numeric value in text

growth_records.cite: Bernstein et al. Indirect Interspecies Regulation: Transcriptional and Physiological Responses of a Cyanobacterium to Heterotrophic Partnership. mSystems (2017). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00181-16. [PMC5340862]
growth_records.snippet: The cultures (binary and axenic) approached 2.4-h doubling times at the highest Ii treatments (2,000 µmol photons m−2 s−1)... maximum specific growth rates (µ) for each condition. Working volume of the reactor (5.5 liters) was held at a con
growth_records.notes: Turbidostat steady state => mu = dilution rate = specific growth rate. Doubling time td=2.4 h reported directly for axenic T. elongatus at Ii=2,000 umol photons m-2 s-1. mu = ln2/td = 0.693147/2.4 = 0.2888 h-1 (rounded 0.289). Temperature 52C and pH 7.5 from Methods. Medium = mBG-11/BP-1 defined medium composition + trace mix listed verbatim from Methods; carbon is inorganic (CO2/HCO3-, photoautotrophic). Specific C-uptake (Cmol basis) and net O2 production rates exist only as figure data (Fig 1A/1B), so numeric qS/qP not extractable from text. O2 sensitivity slope (dmu/dpO2) axenic = 0.127 +/- 0.004 h atm-O2^-1.

growth_records.strain: T. elongatus BP-1 / M. ruber strain A
growth_records.medium: mBG-11 (BP-1 medium), autotrophic (CO2/HCO3- sole C source), nitrate, no vitamins

up.method: specific growth rate on Cmol basis in Fig 1A; heterotrophic partnership had negligible effect on net inorganic C -> biomass conversion vs axenic; numeric values only in figure

sec.method: Fig 1B; specific net O2 production LOWER in binary than axenic control; numeric values only in figure

yields.note: photosynthetic quotient (Cmol into biomass per mol O2) GREATER in binary than axenic (Fig 1C), i.e. binary captured more reductant in biomass; no numeric value in text

growth_records.cite: Bernstein et al. Indirect Interspecies Regulation: Transcriptional and Physiological Responses of a Cyanobacterium to Heterotrophic Partnership. mSystems (2017). DOI:10.1128/mSystems.00181-16. [PMC5340862]
growth_records.snippet: The µ values measured for the binary consortium were nearly identical to those for the T. elongatus axenic control under each Ii treatment. The cultures (binary and axenic) approached 2.4-h doubling times at the highest Ii treatments (2,000
growth_records.notes: Binary consortium doubling time td=2.4 h at Ii=2,000 umol photons m-2 s-1 (stated nearly identical to axenic). mu = ln2/td = 0.693147/2.4 = 0.2888 h-1 (0.289). Turbidostat => mu=dilution rate. Stable species fractions 90.2% T. elongatus / 9.1% M. ruber (cell counts). Specific C-uptake and net O2 production only in figures. O2 sensitivity slope (dmu/dpO2) for binary = 0.086 +/- 0.005 h atm-O2^-1 (lower than axenic 0.127, i.e. reduced O2 sensitivity). Max pO2 treatment = 366 uM dissolved O2 = 281% air saturation at 52C.
